advisories.title: tj-actions/changed-files compromise
advisories.description: Allows for information disclosure of secrets including, but not limited to, valid access keys, GitHub Personal Access Tokens (PATs), npm tokens, and private RSA keys.
recommended_actions: Pin to commit SHA or update to safe tag (changed-files v46.0.1)
Rotate all secrets accessible to affected CI pipelines immediately
Audit downstream artifacts built during the exposure window

advisories.title: Trivy GitHub Actions supply chain compromise
advisories.description: Compromised credentials from a non-atomic credential rotation allowed an attacker to force-push malicious payloads to 76 of 77 trivy-action tags and all setup-trivy tags. The injected code was an infostealer that exfiltrated CI secrets, SSH keys, cloud credentials, and tokens via encrypted channels. Exposure window was approximately 12 hours for trivy-action and 4 hours for setup-trivy starting 2026-03-19 ~17:43 UTC.

indicators.notes: If exfiltration failed and INPUT_GITHUB_PAT was set, the payload created a public repo named tpcp-docs on the victim's GitHub account and uploaded stolen data as a release asset. Check your org for unexpected repos with this name.

recommended_actions: Pin to commit SHA or update to safe tag (trivy-action v0.35.0, setup-trivy v0.2.6)
Rotate all secrets accessible to affected CI pipelines immediately
Audit downstream artifacts built during the exposure window
Check for unauthorized repos named tpcp-docs in your GitHub org
Verify trivy binary integrity — safe versions are v0.69.2 and v0.69.3
